Author: trev5514
Content: I saw a post on CrowdSupply about the different packaging considerations that were evaluated. The design I'm targeting requires 100+ pins, so I can't go with the wire-bonded package. Does anyone have resources on companies that I could get in touch with to get some early quotes? I've taped out through my university, which has had lots of resources, but this is the first one solo, so I don't have much direction on where to look. I'm trying to get a rough price estimate before the design is sent off. Any help is greatly appriciated!

Author: 246tnt
Content: What qty ?

Author: 246tnt
Content: It's a different thing to package 10 samples vs all 1000 dies.

Author: 246tnt
Content:
This was for QFN64 but some prices I got were :
Low qty (~10) : 400 EUR setup + 150 EUR per piece
Larger qty (>=100) : 4kEUR setup + 15 eur per piece.

That's a ballpartk of course. Might be a bit higher with more pins.

Author: trev5514
Content: 50-100 to start, but the ability to repackage later is ideal. It sounds like the upfront setup might be better.

Author: tholin
Content: That’s pretty steep for low qty. After just two or three prototype runs, it’d be more economical to get your own wire bonder and do DIY COB.

Author: 246tnt
Content: Did you find a source for not horribly expensive wire bonders ? 😅

Author: tholin
Content: Going by what others got theirs for

Author: trev5514
Content: @Tholin I'd be interested in that if you have some more resources.

Author: urish
Content: Another datapoint - Two years ago I asked Integra for a quote for 250 QFN-64 packaging, they quoted me $6k.

Author: trev5514
Content: @urish Wow, that's nearly the cost of the tapeout itself! I knew custom packaging was going to be expensive, but this is much higher than I thought.

Author: urish
Content: What I learnt is that this industry is optimized for mass production, not for prototyping

Author: urish
Content: We hope that projects like wafer.space and Tiny Tapeout will make it worthwhile for companies to fill the gap for affordable low-volume packaging solutions

Author: trev5514
Content: Makes sense. It seems like you bite the bullet with extremly low volume (10-30) which is typical for MPW runs, but it's ok since it's such a low volume. Sounds like 1000 chips doesn't really have a place right now so I do hope this will disrupt the market!

Author: mithro_
Content: @Trevor Peyton - My evaluation was that packaging 10 to 100 dies costs *more* than the 1,000 parts using COB.

Author: trev5514
Content: @Tim 'mithro' Ansell That's what I'm finding out. I am going to make some changes and try to fit my design into the proposed wirebond COB!

Author: mithro_
Content: @Trevor Peyton - FYI the costing around COB is around 1-2 cent per wire bond and like 20-30 cent per die + costs of PCB

Author: mithro_
Content: @Trevor Peyton - I'd be open trying to figure out a higher I/O count (>100 pins) option in the future, but that probably is going to be above the $1.50 USD per part currently being offered.

Author: trev5514
Content: @Tim 'mithro' Ansell Heard. More pins is great but I can understand the cost tradeoff. $1.50 per part is pretty unbeatable.

Author: mithro_
Content: Like a high pin count $3 USD option could probably work.

Author: trev5514
Content: ~$3,000 for 1000 chips is still incredible, especially when compared to some of the prices thrown out above.

Author: mithro_
Content: @Trevor Peyton - The hope is eventually it'll just be a standard option as part of PCBA at places like PCBWay / Seeed / JLC PCB / your favorite PCBA house.

Author: trev5514
Content: @Tim 'mithro' Ansell That would be amazing, I hope we can get there!
